name: Marie
classification_notes: Marie is a sans-serif family with a friendly, open character and smooth curves that suggest a humanist influence, designed to be versatile and unobtrusive across a wide range of applications.
summary: A 16-style variable sans-serif from Typejockeys, designed to sit quietly in a layout while offering coverage from Thin to Black with Cyrillic support.
description: A humanist-leaning sans-serif in 16 styles, running from Thin to Black across upright and italic cuts. Available as a variable font, Marie is intended to work without drawing attention to itself, leaving the design breathing room rather than competing with it. Cyrillic support and a set of OpenType features including tabular figures, fractions and arrows extend its utility for multilingual and typographically detailed work.
foundry_blurb: Typejockeys describe Marie as a versatile and unobtrusive sans-serif type family that blends effortlessly into any design, giving layouts room to breathe while delivering clear, timeless typography with a friendly quality. The family spans 16 styles from Thin to Black, including italics, and includes a Cyrillic character set.

name: Aniuk
classification_notes: Aniuk displays a balance of curved and angular forms with a playful yet structured character, consistent with humanist sans design principles rather than strict geometric or grotesque traditions.
summary: A five-weight sans-serif by Thomas Gabriel that balances energetic curves with angular structure, aimed at editorial, poster and logo work.
description: Designed by Thomas Gabriel, Aniuk runs across five weights from Regular to Black, each sharing a tension between fluid curves and harder edges that gives the family a distinctive, characterful feel. OpenType features include alternative glyphs for the y and ampersand, tabular figures and lining figures. The range of weights and the expressive detailing make it well suited to display settings, branding and editorial headlines.
foundry_blurb: Typejockeys describe Aniuk as an original typeface family designed by Thomas Gabriel. Its five weights make it particularly suited to editorial, poster and logo design, with a perfect balance between exciting curves and the necessary edges and corners, giving the family a strong character without sacrificing a certain playfulness.

name: Antonia
classification_notes: Antonia is a contemporary serif with four optical sizes spanning text and display use, exhibiting the contrast variation and proportion shifts characteristic of transitional serif design.
summary: A 46-style serif family with four optical sizes and variable font support, designed by Franziska Weitgruber and Michael Hochleitner at Typejockeys in Austria.
description: Spanning seven weights and four optical sizes from Text through to H1, Antonia adjusts contrast, proportion and detail level for each size range rather than relying on a single design scaled mechanically. The family grew from a phonetic typeface commission for the University of Innsbruck's dialect archive, giving it an unusual scientific origin before being extended into a full retail release. Variable font axes are included with the full family purchase, and OpenType features cover small caps, arrows, fractions and super/subscript figures.
foundry_blurb: Typejockeys describe Antonia as a modern yet down-to-earth type family born in the Alps, comprising 46 fonts across 7 weights and 4 optical sizes, with text, display and italic styles optimised for every size and application. The family originated in a commission for the University of Innsbruck, where a phonetic script font was developed for a digital dialect atlas of Tyrolean communities. The full family purchase includes dual-axis variable fonts at no extra cost.

name: Carabelle
classification_notes: Carabelle presents as a casual, handwritten script typeface with a single Regular style, OpenType alternates and decorative features typical of display script fonts.
summary: A casual handwritten script with a single style, offering OpenType alternates, arrows and decorative underlines for display use.
description: A single-weight casual script designed for display settings such as packaging, greeting cards and cafe signage. OpenType features include alternative letterforms, arrows, fractions and underline variants, giving designers flexibility within a single font file. The specimen examples span food branding, seasonal cards and event graphics, suggesting a warm, approachable register.
foundry_blurb: Typejockeys present Carabelle as a one-style script font with a range of OpenType features including alternative forms, arrows, fractions and underlines, demonstrated across contexts such as café branding, Christmas cards and festive event graphics.

name: Arnika
classification_notes: Arnika presents crisp, refined letterforms with fine detailing consistent with a transitional serif tradition, positioned for fashion and beauty contexts where elegance and precision are expected.
summary: A four-width serif family by Michael Hochleitner, built for fashion and beauty publishing with crisp detailing across Regular, SemiCondensed, Condensed and Compressed cuts.
description: Designed by Michael Hochleitner, Arnika offers four width variants from Regular through to Compressed, giving designers flexibility across single-column and tight editorial layouts. Fine, precise details lend the typeface a confident elegance without sacrificing delicacy, making it well suited to beauty products, fashion magazines and luxury branding. A variable font is included free with a complete family licence, and OpenType features include small caps, arrows, superior and inferior figures and contextual alternates.
foundry_blurb: Typejockeys describe Arnika as a charming type family designed by Michael Hochleitner, offered in four widths — Regular, SemiCondensed, Condensed and ExtraCondensed — to bring flexibility and diversity to your work. Crisp details convey confidence without losing fineness, and while they consider it a natural fit for beauty products and fashion magazines, they leave its application to the user's imagination. A variable font is included free with a complete family licence.

name: Firelli
classification_notes: Firelli is a serif family with refined details suited to both display and text use; its mention of incorruptible headline details and solid text posture places it in the contemporary humanist or transitional serif tradition.
summary: A 14-style serif family by Teddy Derkert, spanning Thin to Black with italics, swash caps and variable font support, aimed at editorial and magazine work.
description: Designed by Teddy Derkert and produced by Typejockeys, Firelli covers seven weights from Thin to Black, each with a matching italic. OpenType features include swash caps, small caps, ligatures, circled numbers, and inferior and superior figures. The family is positioned for content-heavy editorial projects such as magazines and annual reports, with fine headline details and enough range to sustain longer reading text.
foundry_blurb: Typejockeys describe Firelli as an original family of 14 styles with 7 weights and italics, featuring italic swash caps, ligatures and alternate characters. They suggest it suits big headlines thanks to its precise details, while longer texts benefit from its wide weight range and solid posture, recommending it for content-packed magazines, visual annual reports and bedtime stories. A variable font is included free when the complete family is licensed.

name: Freude
classification_notes: Freude is a display typeface with a playful, rounded character suited to signage, entertainment and branding contexts, as evidenced by its use with Haribo and funland park copy.
summary: A cheerful display sans by Thomas Gabriel, built for entertainment and consumer branding with a warm, rounded character.
description: Designed by Thomas Gabriel for Typejockeys, Freude is a display typeface with a convivial personality that suits funfairs, food brands and retail signage, as demonstrated by its use for Haribo and similar consumer clients. It ships in a single Regular weight and includes a suite of OpenType features: small caps, arrows, circled numbers and fractions. The name itself means 'joy' in German, and the design carries that brief through in its rounded, approachable forms.
foundry_blurb: Typejockeys present Freude as a display typeface designed by Thomas Gabriel, shown in use for brands such as Nestlé, Haribo and Strizzi Cola, with OpenType features including small caps, arrows, circled numbers and fractions.

name: Gretel Script
designer: Michael Hochleitner, Christoph Schütz, Simon Liesinger, Franziska Weitgruber
classification_notes: Based on the handwriting of calligrapher Natascha Safarik and redrawn into vectors; the three optical sizes and OpenType alternates place it firmly in the calligraphic script category with a quirky, informal character.
summary: A calligraphic script in three optical sizes, based on the handwriting of calligrapher Natascha Safarik and redrawn manually for clean vectors at any size.
description: The family spans three styles — Piccolo, the standard cut, and Grande — each tuned to a different size range so letterforms remain balanced whether set small or large. OpenType features include alternates, initial and final forms, intentional breaks and perfect connections, giving composers fine control over the flow of joined lettering. Its quirky, expressive character has been applied to wedding stationery, public campaigns and food packaging.
foundry_blurb: Typejockeys describe Gretel Script as a quirky script packed with features, with optical sizes as its standout quality. The three-style family is drawn from the calligraphy of Natascha Safarik, with all glyphs redrawn manually to produce vector shapes that look perfect at any size. Illustrations for the family are by Frau Isa.

name: Henriette
classification_notes: Henriette draws from Viennese street sign lettering of the 1920s, a tradition rooted in heavy display serifs with condensed variants. The family's bold, upright character and its origins in signage typography place it firmly in display territory.
summary: Drawn from the fragmented legacy of 1920s Viennese street sign lettering, Henriette synthesises 16 surviving variants into a single coherent display family across three widths.
description: Rather than digitising any single surviving version, Michael Hochleitner studied the full range of Viennese street sign typefaces that emerged from successive redrawings by enamel manufacturers, then distilled them into one family of 31 styles. The family spans Normal, Condensed and Compressed widths, each available in multiple weights with fully original italics designed to accompany the roman. OpenType features include ligatures, swash caps, stylistic alternates and figure styles, giving it considerable range beyond its signage origins.
foundry_blurb: In the 1920s, the Viennese government standardised its street signs using a purpose-made typeface. Over time, enamel factories redrew it independently, producing 16 distinct and sometimes significantly different versions. Henriette takes inspiration from all of them rather than digitising any one, with fully original italics added to accompany the roman.

name: Sauber Script
classification_notes: A connected script typeface with Cyrillic support including Bulgarian and Serbian alternates, suggesting a cursive handwritten style suitable for packaging and branding contexts.
summary: A connected script with Cyrillic coverage, including Bulgarian and Serbian alternates, aimed at packaging and identity work.
description: Sauber Script is a connected cursive typeface from Typejockeys offering OpenType alternates for Bulgarian, Serbian and standard Cyrillic forms. The single Regular weight suits applications where a handwritten warmth is needed, from food packaging to brand identity. Its real-world usage across clients such as Saubermacher and Mautner Markhof points to a typeface comfortable in commercial and retail contexts.
foundry_blurb: Typejockeys present Sauber Script as a connected script typeface with OpenType support for Bulgarian, Serbian and Cyrillic alternates, shown in use across a range of branding and packaging projects.

name: Ingeborg
classification_notes: Ingeborg is a display serif with expressive weight contrast and decorative variants including a striped style, placing it firmly in the display serif category rather than text-optimised roman traditions.
summary: A ten-style display serif from Typejockeys with a wide weight range from Regular to Fat, including a decorative striped variant.
description: Ten styles span four weights from Regular to Fat, each with a matching italic, plus a novelty Striped cut that gives the family a decorative dimension. OpenType features include swash caps, figure styles, ligatures and superior and inferior figures, making it capable enough for editorial and identity work. The weight extremes at Heavy and Fat lean into poster and display applications where strong typographic colour is needed.
foundry_blurb: Typejockeys present Ingeborg as a display serif family available in 10 styles, including Regular, Bold, Heavy, Fat and their italics, alongside a decorative Striped variant. The family supports OpenType features such as swash caps, multiple figure styles and ligatures, and has been used by clients including Vorwerk and in editorial contexts.
